subject: Economics
chapter: Forms of Market and Price Determination

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Monopolistic competition is a market structure where a single firm has a significant degree of control over the market.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Monopolistic competition is characterized by a large number of firms producing differentiated products and each firm has some degree of control over the market but not a significant degree of control.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Monopolistic competition is characterized by a large number of firms producing differentiated products and each firm has some degree of control over the market but not a significant degree of control.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): In perfect competition, firms are price-takers and they do not have any control over the price of the product.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Firms in perfect competition produce homogeneous products and have no market power to influence the price.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Firms in perfect competition are price-takers because they produce homogeneous products and have no market power to influence the price.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Monopoly is a market structure where a single firm supplies the entire market with a particular product.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Monopoly is characterized by a single firm having complete control over the market and supply of the product.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Monopoly is a market structure where a single firm supplies the entire market with a particular product and has complete control over the market and supply of the product.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Oligopoly is a market structure where a few large firms compete with each other.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Oligopoly is characterized by a small number of firms producing differentiated products and competing with each other.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Oligopoly is a market structure where a small number of firms producing differentiated products and competing with each other.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Price ceiling is a government imposed price limit on a product.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Price ceiling is a price-fixing mechanism used by the government to protect consumers from price gouging.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Price ceiling is a government imposed price limit on a product to protect consumers from price gouging.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Perfect competition is a market structure where firms produce homogeneous products and there is free entry and exit.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Perfect competition is characterized by a large number of firms, free entry and exit, and homogeneous products.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Perfect competition is a market structure where firms produce homogeneous products and there is free entry and exit.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Monopoly is a market structure where a single firm has complete control over the market.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Monopoly is characterized by a single firm having a patent or copyright that prevents other firms from producing the product.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Monopoly is a market structure where a single firm has complete control over the market, but it can be due to various factors like patent, copyright, etc.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Price floor is a government imposed minimum price on a product.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Price floor is a price-fixing mechanism used by the government to protect farmers from low prices.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Price floor is a government imposed minimum price on a product to protect farmers from low prices.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Oligopoly is a market structure where firms produce homogeneous products.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Oligopoly is characterized by a small number of firms producing differentiated products.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Oligopoly is a market structure where firms produce differentiated products, not homogeneous products.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): In monopoly, the demand curve is perfectly elastic.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Monopoly is characterized by a single firm having complete control over the market and supply of the product.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: In monopoly, the demand curve is not perfectly elastic, it is downward sloping due to the single firm having complete control over the market and supply of the product.
